titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lewis B. Cullman Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Robert Bartels

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 13, 2020 at 07:11

did.abstract: The collection documents the business and philanthropic activities of Lewis B. Cullman. Business records include business dealings with the Allied Graphic Arts Company and Cullman's leveraged buyout of the Orkin Exterminating Company in the 1960s. Also included are two bound volumes documenting the merger of Cullman's company, Cullman Ventures, Inc., with Mead Acquisition Corporation in 1999. Personal papers include biographical information, documents relating to Cullman's relationship with Yale University, records of his philanthropic contributions to New York institutions, and publicity materials for organizations which Cullman founded or contributed to. The collection also includes audiovisual recordings documenting programs funded by Cullman, interviews, and publicity materials about Cullman and his charitable contributions. There are also photographs of Cullman taken at organizations and events related to his philanthropy.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lewis B. Cullman is a New York City businessman and philanthropist. Born in 1919, Cullman received a BA from Yale and a master's degree in meteorology from New York University. In his business career, Cullman served as the chief executive of the At-A-Glance calendar and appointment-book company, and in 1964 engineered the purchase of the Orkin Exterminating Company. After selling his company in 1999, Cullman became a philanthropist. He serves on the boards of the New York Public Library, the Museum of Modern Art, and the New York Botanical Garden. He is also involved with Chess in the Schools, a program which teaches chess to New York Public School students, and is the founder of the Cullman Child Development Center, in Sidney, NY. Cullman was thrice married, most recently in 2010 to Louise Hirschfeld. His second wife, Dorothy, played a large role in his philanthropic activites.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in three groupings: Business records, Personal papers, and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the business and philanthropic activities of Lewis B. Cullman. Business records include business dealings with the Allied Graphic Arts Company and Cullman's leveraged buyout of the Orkin Exterminating Company in the 1960s. Also included are two bound volumes documenting the merger of Cullman's company, Cullman Ventures, Inc., with Mead Acquisition Corporation in 1999. Personal papers include biographical information, documents relating to Cullman's relationship with Yale University, records of his philanthropic contributions to New York institutions, and publicity materials for organizations which Cullman founded or contributed to. The collection also includes audiovisual recordings documenting programs funded by Cullman, interviews, and publicity materials about Cullman and his charitable contributions. There are also photographs of Cullman taken at organizations and events related to his philanthropy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of the creator(s), or until January 1, 2027, whichever comes first, copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lewis B. Cullman Papers (MS 2072). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

corpname.part: Science, Industry and Business Library (New York Public Library)
New York Public Library for the Performing Arts

did.unittitle: Merger of Cullman Ventures, Inc. and Mead Acquisition Corp. Volume 1

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

The computer files are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu . The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest including collection, box, and folder numbers (or folder descriptions if folders are not numbered). The review may take several weeks.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access
